message:
fix(storage): retain flock file handle to maintain OS-level lock (br-21qq)

The FileLock struct acquired an exclusive flock via try_lock_exclusive()
but immediately dropped the File handle when it went out of scope,
releasing the OS-level lock. Only the in-process held flag persisted.

Fix: Added lock_file: Option<fs::File> field to FileLock. The file
handle is stored on successful lock acquisition and dropped in release(),
maintaining the OS-level flock for the entire lock duration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1bm","title":"HTTP Server Parity: Legacy Python Exact Match","description":"## Objective
Port the **HTTP server stack** to Rust with exact legacy Python behavior: auth, rate limiting, streamable HTTP transport, mail UI endpoints, logging, and background workers.

## Scope
- Middleware: bearer auth, JWT + RBAC, rate limiting.
- Streamable HTTP MCP transport (stateless ASGI adapter behavior, header normalization, base path mounting).
- Mail UI routes and static assets.
- Request logging + OTEL no‑op parity.
- Background workers (ack TTL, file reservation cleanup, tool metrics, retention/quota).
- Health + well‑known endpoints.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests per subsystem (JWT, rate limit, streamable HTTP, mail UI, logging, workers).
- E2E HTTP suite (br-2ei.9.6) covering auth, CORS, streamable, and UI endpoints.

## Logging/Artifacts
- HTTP transcripts + diffs under `tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:32.057076856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646906905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646884493Z","close_reason":"All HTTP server parity child beads complete (auth/JWT/RBAC, rate limit, peer addr/localhost bypass, streamable HTTP, /mail SSR UI, CORS, logging+OTEL no-op, workers, health/well-known)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:48:14.330942304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-36w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:23.374551664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1","title":"JWT Auth Parity (HS256 + JWKS + role claim)","description":"Objective:
Implement JWT authentication exactly as in legacy Python (mcp_agent_mail/http.py). This includes HS256 HMAC and JWKS verification paths, audience/issuer validation, role extraction, and error semantics. Must be 1:1 on outputs and failure modes.

Legacy Reference Behavior (must match):
- If JWT enabled and Authorization missing or not Bearer: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Decode token using:
  - If JWKS URL set: fetch JWKS, select key by header.kid, verify
  - Else if secret set: verify with HMAC secret
  - Algorithms from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default HS256)
- Validate audience/issuer if configured; otherwise ignore
- On any failure: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Extract roles from claim HTTP_JWT_ROLE_CLAIM (default "role")
  - String -> singleton set
  - List/tuple -> set of strings
  - Else -> empty
- If roles empty, use HTTP_RBAC_DEFAULT_ROLE
- Store claims for rate limit identity (sub) and any downstream uses

Implementation Considerations:
- JWKS fetch must be async-safe via asupersync HTTP client (no tokio)
- Cache JWKS by URL with TTL to avoid per-request latency
- Must not change external response schema or status codes
- Log auth failures at debug/trace (no extra HTTP output)

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
Unit tests:
- Missing Authorization header -> 401
- Non-Bearer Authorization -> 401
- HS256 valid/invalid signatures
- JWKS valid/invalid (kid missing, bad signature)
- Audience/issuer mismatch -> 401
- Role claim variants (string, list, missing)
Integration tests:
- Start server with HTTP_JWT_* env vars; make requests with valid/invalid JWT
- Verify RBAC path uses roles correctly
E2E:
- Covered by `br-1bm.1.5` (uses shared harness `br-2ei.9.1`).

Notes:
- Localhost bypass logic is owned by `br-1bm.3` + `br-1bm.8`; JWT must still match legacy in the non-bypass path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JWT behavior matches legacy for all vectors extracted in `br-1bm.1.1`.
- Role extraction matches Python behavior across all supported claim shapes.
- `br-1bm.1.5` unit+integration+E2E suite passes and produces high-signal artifacts on failure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperBarn","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:46.671284098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957613837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T13:29:42.957588740Z","close_reason":"All subtasks br-1bm.1.1-1.5 complete; JWT unit+integration+E2E coverage passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1.1","title":"JWT Parity: extract legacy behavior + test vectors","description":"Purpose:
Extract exact legacy JWT behavior and define concrete test vectors so Rust matches Python 1:1.

Legacy Implementation Notes (from http.py):
- Header parse: token.split(".",1)[0]; base64url decode with padding; JSON parse. Any error -> None.
- If jwks_url set:
  - httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5) GET jwks_url -> .json()
  - JsonWebKey.import_key_set(jwks)
  - kid = header.get("kid"); key = find_by_kid(kid) if present else key_set.keys[0]
- Else if secret set:
  - JsonWebKey.import_key(secret, {"kty":"oct"})
- If key None -> Unauthorized.
- jwt.decode(token, key) using algs from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default ["HS256"]). Any error -> Unauthorized.
- If audience configured: claims.validate_aud(audience)
- If issuer configured: if str(claims.get("iss") or "") != issuer -> Unauthorized (no fallback)
- claims.validate() (handles exp/nbf/iat if present)
- Returns dict(claims) on success.

Concrete Test Vectors (must implement):
1) Missing Authorization header -> 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
2) Authorization not Bearer -> 401
3) Header parse fail (malformed base64 / non-JSON header) -> 401
4) JWKS path:
   - jwks_url set, kid present, matching key -> success
   - jwks_url set, kid present, no matching key -> 401
   - jwks_url set, kid missing -> uses first key in key_set
   - jwks fetch fails / invalid JSON -> 401
5) Secret path:
   - jwt_secret set, HS256 valid -> success
   - jwt_secret set, signature invalid -> 401
6) Algorithms:
   - alg not in HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS -> 401
7) Audience:
   - jwt_audience set, token aud matches -> success
   - jwt_audience set, token aud mismatch -> 401
8) Issuer:
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss matches exactly -> success
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss missing or different -> 401
9) Claims validate:
   - exp in past -> 401
   - nbf in future -> 401
   - exp/nbf absent -> ok
10) Role claim:
   - role claim string -> roles={string}
   - role claim list -> roles={strings}
   - role claim missing/invalid -> roles empty -> default role applied downstream

Deliverable:
- A markdown or JSON matrix of inputs -> expected HTTP status/body and roles extracted.
- Must be explicit enough that Rust tests can be derived directly.

Dependencies: none (spec extraction only).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:09.570324403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060998320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060980056Z","close_reason":"Extracted legacy JWT behavior + explicit test vectors in bead description","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1r9v","title":"Console layout config: HUD sizing + persistence","description":"Add interactive console layout configuration allowing users to control HUD size/position vs scrolling log output, with automatic persistence.

USER NEED:
- Configure how much screen real estate the static HUD occupies vs the scrolling log.
  - Examples: bottom 20%, bottom 50%.
- Do this interactively and have settings remembered.

DEFAULTS (must be great out-of-the-box):
- Rich HUD starts automatically in a real TTY when `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` (default).
- Default HUD size is ~33% of terminal height, anchored bottom.

CONFIGURABLE SETTINGS (env vars + persisted user config):
1) `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT`: u8 (10-80, default 33)
   - Maps to: `ui_height = (term_height * pct / 100).max(8).min(term_height - 2)`

2) `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR`: enum {bottom, top} (default bottom)
   - Maps to `ftui::UiAnchor`

3) `CONSOLE_UI_AUTO_SIZE`: bool (default false)
   - When true: use `ScreenMode::InlineAuto { min_height, max_height }` instead of fixed.

4) `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE`: bool (default true)
   - When true, interactive adjustments persist immediately.

PERSISTENCE FILE:
- `~/.config/agent-mail/console.toml`
  - Priority: env vars > console.toml > defaults
  - Saving should preserve stable formatting (don’t churn on every save).

INTERACTIVE ADJUSTMENT:
- HTTP server mode only (stdio mode uses stdin for MCP protocol).
- Spawn a lightweight `/dev/tty` (or stdin) reader thread when running in a real TTY:
  - Up / '+': increase `ui_height_percent` by 5 (max 80)
  - Down / '-': decrease `ui_height_percent` by 5 (min 10)
  - 't'/'b': toggle anchor top/bottom
  - 'a': toggle auto-size
  - 's': force-save settings
  - 'q': disable interactive adjustments for this run
- HUD should display a subtle, dim hint line when interactive mode is active:
  - `+/- resize | t/b anchor | a autosize | s save`

INTEGRATION NOTES:
- Left/right splits ("left 30%") require AltScreen + an internal log viewer.
  - That’s implemented separately in `br-1m6a.19`.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add config fields to `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` + `from_env()` parsing.
- Add load/save for `console.toml` (no new global config system; just this file).
- Modify `StartupDashboard::maybe_start()` to use configured values instead of hardcoded 1/3.

TESTING:
- Config loading priority (env > file > defaults)
- `ui_height` calculation + clamp behavior
- Anchor parsing fallback
- Save/load roundtrip for console.toml
- Interactive key handling logic (unit test the state machine; no real tty required)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Users can resize HUD interactively in HTTP mode and it persists.
- Defaults remain enabled and sane with zero config.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:18:52.502187876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839103222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839080589Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of br-1m6a.19 (merged console layout plan there).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"br-1t0e","title":"Resources: file_reservations resource should be bounded (avoid glob explosion)","description":"## Problem
`resource://file_reservations/{slug}` currently expands glob patterns via filesystem enumeration and may run `git log` over many pathspecs. On large projects with broad patterns (e.g. `src/**`), this can take >60s and effectively wedges the MCP server (subsequent tool/resource calls time out).

## Objective
Make `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` reliably fast and non-blocking for large repos while keeping legacy output shape.

## Scope
- Avoid enumerating all filesystem matches for glob patterns.
- Compute git activity without expanding to thousands of explicit paths (use git pathspec/glob or prefix-based strategy).
- Bound work per pattern (time/size caps) and fail open by returning `null` activity timestamps when exceeding caps.
- Ensure cleanup logic (TTL expiry + stale release) cannot monopolize the server request thread.

## Tests
- Unit tests covering glob-heavy patterns (`src/**`, `crates/**`, `**/*.rs`) ensure the code path does not enumerate full repo trees (use a temp dir with many files to assert completes quickly).
- Regression test: resource handler completes within a small time budget under a synthetic repo with many files and a broad glob reservation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resource://file_reservations/...` returns within a bounded time on large repos (no tool server wedge).
2. Output shape remains compatible (fields present, timestamps may be null when capped).
3. Existing conformance tests still pass; add targeted tests for the new caps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:05.771837055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745259737Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745241343Z","close_reason":"Avoid glob explosion in resource://file_reservations: stop filesystem glob expansion; use git pathspec glob magic; add regression test; ran fmt+clippy+test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1t0e","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:34.586726901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-21gj.6.2","title":"Publish migration guide for current CLI users","description":"Background:\nCurrent CLI users need a precise migration path to avoid workflow breakage and confusion.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Migration guide with before/after command mappings and mode-switch instructions.\n- Automation/script migration section including environment/config precedence pitfalls.\n- Troubleshooting section keyed to common denial or mismatch symptoms.\n\nValidation requirements:\n- Example migrations are exercised via e2e scripts and produce expected outputs.\n- Guide references concrete artifacts from br-21gj.5.6/5.7 for reproducibility.\n\nDefinition of done:\nA user can migrate existing automation without consulting historical planning documents.","notes":"Non-overlap: migration-only narrative; baseline docs are br-21gj.6.1 and rollout controls are br-21gj.6.3.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"OrangeFinch","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:44.602393696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:57.951897477Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:57.951812659Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-21gj.6.2","depends_on_id":"br-21gj.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:48.273434423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-21gj.6.2","depends_on_id":"br-21gj.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:44.602393696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-21gj.6.2","depends_on_id":"br-21gj.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:48.222148891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-21gj.6.3","title":"Rollout + kill-switch playbook for dual-mode launch","description":"Background:\nDual-mode rollout needs explicit operational controls to avoid user-visible incidents.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Phased rollout plan with activation criteria and blast-radius constraints.\n- Kill-switch/fallback procedure with role ownership and communication protocol.\n- Runbook checks tied to e2e and CI artifacts before each rollout phase.\n\nValidation requirements:\n- Dry-run simulation of rollback path and decision points.\n- Traceability from rollout gates to br-21gj.5.6 and br-21gj.5.7 evidence.\n\nDefinition of done:\nOps can execute launch/rollback safely with deterministic criteria and documented responsibilities.","notes":"Non-overlap: this bead is deployment operations; release checklist finalization remains br-21gj.6.4.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayForge","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:44.662477580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:55:30.955196682Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:55:30.955116021Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-21gj.6.3","depends_on_id":"br-21gj.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:38.659930035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-21gj.6.3","depends_on_id":"br-21gj.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:44.662477580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-21gj.6.4","title":"Release checklist + post-release monitoring checklist updates","description":"Background:\nRelease quality depends on explicit verification and post-release monitoring discipline.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Release checklist covering mode resolution, denial UX, CLI command-family behavior, and artifact sanity checks.\n- Post-release monitoring checklist with concrete signals (error classes, bypass attempts, user confusion patterns).\n- Escalation and triage mapping from observed signal to responsible runbook action.\n\nValidation requirements:\n- Checklist steps are executable and validated against current CI/e2e artifacts.\n- Monitoring checks include sample queries/dashboards and expected ranges.\n\nDefinition of done:\nRelease/post-release procedures are actionable, complete, and tightly linked to tested behavior.","notes":"Non-overlap: operational checklist layer only;  {"id":"br-27m","title":"Share: search indexes + materialized views + perf indexes","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 4–6: FTS5 search indexes, materialized views, and performance indexes for static viewer.

## Scope
- **build_search_indexes**: create `fts_messages` virtual table; populate with subject/body/importance/project/thread_key/created_ts; optimize.
- **build_materialized_views**: create `message_overview_mv`, `attachments_by_message_mv`, `fts_search_overview_mv` (if FTS enabled).
- **create_performance_indexes**: add lowercase columns and covering indexes for fast viewer filtering.

## Tests
- Unit tests for FTS presence/absence path.
- Integration tests verifying view row counts and index existence.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store schema dumps under `tests/artifacts/share/indexes/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. FTS and MV tables match legacy schemas and row counts.
2. Performance indexes created without errors, idempotent.
3.
